narrative: Markets sold off for a second straight week as an escalating U.S.-Israel/Iran conflict pushed oil sharply higher, reviving inflation fears and driving a risk-off rotation with energy the lone bright spot. A weak February jobs report (unexpected payroll decline and higher unemployment) added stagflation worries, reinforcing the drawdown in small caps and cyclicals into Friday’s close.

value_picks.name: Sanofi ADR
value_picks.why_undervalued: Trading at/near a 52-week low during 2026-03-06’s session, the stock reflected investor skepticism on pharma pipeline/patent cycles despite Sanofi’s diversified vaccine and specialty care base.
value_picks.bull_case: Multiple product launches plus steady vaccine cash flows can support earnings durability; mean reversion toward global pharma multiples could re-rate the ADR if pipeline execution improves.

value_picks.name: BXP, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Hit a 52-week low around 2026-03-06 amid office-REIT pessimism, even as core rent collections continued to generate operating cash flow.
value_picks.bull_case: If office utilization stabilizes and rates ease, asset values and leasing spreads can recover; equity could re-rate off depressed NAV multiples while cash flow supports the payout.

value_picks.name: Builders FirstSource
value_picks.why_undervalued: Also listed among 52-week lows on 2026-03-06 as housing-cycle fears dominated despite scale advantages and strong cash generation in prior cycles.
value_picks.bull_case: A housing repair/remodel floor plus eventual new-build recovery could drive operating leverage; continued buybacks at low multiples can amplify EPS rebound.

value_picks.name: Full Truck Alliance Co. ADR
value_picks.why_undervalued: Printed a 52-week low on 2026-03-06 as China/ADR sentiment stayed weak despite an asset-light marketplace model that historically throws off cash.
value_picks.bull_case: Freight-cycle normalization and higher monetization/penetration could lift margins; any easing in China tech/ADR risk premium could re-rate valuation.

value_picks.name: Comcast Corp.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Large-cap communication-services laggard trading near yearly lows in early 2026 as broadband growth slowed and investors discounted legacy cable economics.
value_picks.bull_case: Stabilizing broadband adds, disciplined capital returns, and optionality from NBCU/streaming and theme parks can support a rerating from single-digit multiples.

momentum_picks.name: SanDisk Corp.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Strong AI/server storage demand narrative and continued leadership among 2026 top performers, with technical setup described as a sustained uptrend into March 2026.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: High momentum but vulnerable to a sharp pullback if AI capex sentiment cools or guidance disappoints.

momentum_picks.name: Moderna, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Re-rating on pipeline/newsflow with the stock cited among 2026’s best S&P 500 performers by mid-March, reflecting sustained upside momentum into early March.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Biotech sentiment and trial outcomes can swing sharply, so position sizing and volatility tolerance matter.

momentum_picks.name: Texas Pacific Land
momentum_picks.catalyst: Permian-exposed royalty/land business stayed among S&P 500’s top YTD performers in 2026, supported by higher oil prices into March.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Momentum is tied to commodity prices; a reversal in crude could compress multiples quickly.

momentum_picks.name: Western Digital
momentum_picks.catalyst: Storage and data-center cycle upswing (AI infrastructure) drove strong relative strength, with WDC cited among best-performing stocks lists for 2026.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Cyclical memory/storage pricing can reverse; upside depends on sustained tight supply and demand.

momentum_picks.name: Vertiv Holdings
momentum_picks.catalyst: AI data-center power/thermal infrastructure theme remained a key driver of strong multi-month performance into early March 2026.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Valuation and execution risk rise after big runs; any capex pause by hyperscalers could trigger de-rating.

watchlist_updates.detail: Raised FY guidance to $11B on AI demand; shares surged despite market decline.
Nearing 500 737 Max order announcement tied to Trump China visit.
Q2 EPS $4.58 rev $69.6B beat estimates; defensive play.
Energy sector up as WTI +30% to $90 on Iran conflict.
Benefited from record weekly oil gain amid supply fears.
Industrials down 2.2% on growth fears from weak jobs data.
Banks pressured by higher oil/inflation reducing rate cut odds.
Mag7 mixed but software underperformed amid risk-off.
Held up better than peers despite Nasdaq drop.
EV sensitive to economic slowdown signals from jobs miss.
